activity.title: AI-powered defense for an AI-accelerated threat landscape
activity.raw_text: Recent advances in AI model capabilities are changing how vulnerabilities are discovered and exploited. AI models can autonomously discover weaknesses, chain multiple lower-severity issues into working end-to-end exploits, and produce working proof-of-concept code. This significantly compresses the window between vulnerability discovery and exploitation. These changes require organizations to rethink exposure, response, and risk. However, the same capabilities that can give attackers an advantage also create a unique opportunity for defenders.

_verify_phrases: compresses the window between vulnerability discovery and exploitation

activity.title: Putting AI to work with the building trades
activity.raw_text: At Microsoft, we are honored to partner with North America's Building Trades Unions (NABTU) to invest in the people who build with us. Today we are announcing an expanded partnership to support a strong, skilled workforce pipeline and help workers across North America build the skills needed to succeed in an AI-powered economy. We believe that the North American skilled trades workforce is one of the most talented workforce systems. This week, thousands of these talented workers from across North America gather in Washington, DC for their annual Legislative Conference.

_verify_phrases: honored to partner with North America's Building Trades Unions

activity.title: Introducing the Agent Governance Toolkit: Open-source runtime security for AI agents
activity.raw_text: In December 2025, OWASP published the Top 10 for Agentic Applications for 2026, the first formal taxonomy of risks specific to autonomous AI agents, including goal hijacking, tool misuse, identity abuse, memory poisoning, cascading failures, and rogue agents. Regulatory frameworks are following: the European Union AI Act's high-risk AI obligations take effect in August 2026, and the Colorado AI Act becomes enforceable in June 2026. Today, we're sharing the Agent Governance Toolkit, an open-source project released under the Microsoft organization and MIT license that brings runtime security governance to autonomous AI agents.

_verify_phrases: first toolkit to address all 10 OWASP agentic AI risks

activity.title: Celebrating 250 million: Empowering communities to enable the global AI economy
activity.raw_text: Building on years of progress, Microsoft is evolving its approach to digital access to focus not only on coverage, but on adoption, enablement, and long-term participation in the AI economy. As part of this next chapter, we are announcing a new collaboration with Starlink. This collaboration expands the set of tools available to help deliver digital access in rural, agricultural, and hard-to-reach communities. Combined with local delivery partners and community institutions, it strengthens the foundation for AI-ready communities around the world.

_verify_phrases: we are announcing a new collaboration with Starlink

activity.title: Brad Smith: U.S. tech should 'worry a little' about Chinese AI subsidies
activity.raw_text: Microsoft President Brad Smith said American companies will have to compete with subsidies provided by Beijing to Chinese companies. "I do think we always have to think about, maybe even worry a little bit about Chinese subsidies," Smith said. There is a growing view that China's lower-cost AI models could prove to be attractive in developing nations. Smith said subsidies from Beijing to Chinese companies were "the fundamental approach that China successfully took to disrupt the telecommunications market," when state money and support helped companies like Huawei and ZTE expand.

_verify_phrases: worry a little bit about Chinese subsidies

activity.title: We need to act with urgency to address the growing AI divide
activity.raw_text: Microsoft announces at the India AI Impact Summit it is on pace to invest USD $50 billion by the end of the decade to help bring AI to countries across the Global South. Artificial intelligence is diffusing at an impressive speed, but its adoption around the world remains profoundly uneven. As Microsoft's latest AI Diffusion Report shows, AI usage in the Global North is roughly twice that of the Global South. And this divide continues to widen.

_verify_phrases: AI usage in the Global North is roughly twice that of the Global South
